education-oxford.title: Oxford Summer School
education-oxford.subtitle: Knowledge Representation, Aug. 2018
education-oxford.summary: A short academic branch at Oriel College that widened the undergraduate horizon before the later research-heavy years.

detail.text: The old resume lists this as a summer school in knowledge representation at Oriel College, Oxford.

education-hust.title: HUST Electronic and Information Engineering
education-hust.subtitle: Bachelor's degree, 2016-2020
education-hust.summary: The undergraduate base where software, computer vision, modeling, and student-organization work first started to braid together.

detail.text: I graduated in the top 10 with a 3.91/4.0 GPA. Research, contests, design work, and course projects gradually pulled the path toward machine learning and software.

education-cornell.title: Cornell M.Eng. in ECE
education-cornell.subtitle: Graduate study in Ithaca
education-cornell.summary: The Cornell period connected data visualization, machine learning, systems, and the path from internship to full-time work at Verily.

detail.text: Coursework and projects stretched across visualization, vision, and systems. Life in Ithaca also opened into travel and, later, work in California.

research-signature-attack.title: Signature Verification Attack
research-signature-attack.subtitle: Black-box adversarial ML research
research-signature-attack.summary: A research thread on region-restricted black-box attacks against handwritten signature verification that later became a Pattern Recognition paper.

detail.text: The work started from a course interest in adversarial examples. It focused on keeping perturbations inside the stroke region, so they stayed hard to notice and survived background cleaning.

items: Started in 2019 in Wei Yuan's lab

research-noise-modulation.title: Noise Modulation
research-noise-modulation.subtitle: Interpretability and robustness study
research-noise-modulation.summary: An unpublished paper proposing a lightweight way to recover cleaner, more interpretable input gradients without full adversarial training.

detail.text: The idea was to multiply images with learned noise during training. That pushed the model to rely on the truly critical components, cleaning up the gradient signal at the cost of some accuracy.

items: Cheap alternative to one aspect of adversarial training
Interesting result, but not publishable in its current form

project-wellmail.title: WELLMAIL
project-wellmail.subtitle: Python e-mail client
project-wellmail.summary: A first serious software project: a Python e-mail client with a GUI, built as a team course project and remembered as an early step into real software construction.

detail.text: The main lesson was not only the client itself. It was learning how to divide a graphical software system into modules and build something with a real interface flow.

items: Receive Box and Main Module ownership
One of the strongest projects in the class cohort

project-louvre-evacuation.title: Louvre Evacuation Model
project-louvre-evacuation.subtitle: ICM 2019 modeling project
project-louvre-evacuation.summary: A contest modeling project that used a cellular-automata-style abstraction of the Louvre to design a dynamic evacuation strategy.

detail.text: Instead of modeling each person as a cell, the system modeled representative exhibition halls. It then simulated how congestion-aware routing could accelerate evacuation.

project-weibe.title: WEIBE
project-weibe.subtitle: Weibo emotion analysis system
project-weibe.summary: A course-built system that crawled Weibo trending topics, stored posts, and visualized emotional tendencies across those conversations.

detail.text: The main contribution was system design: a Selenium crawler, MongoDB-backed storage, Python server work, and a simple frontend for exploring the results.

items: Capstone Project in Big Data Processing System
Captured topics during the early COVID-19 moment

project-faceswap.title: FaceSwap Deepfake System
project-faceswap.subtitle: Undergraduate capstone project
project-faceswap.summary: A bachelor's graduation project focused on generating face-swapped short videos, with attention to the full pipeline rather than only the core model.

detail.text: The core work centered on the intracoder and color-correction modules. The broader challenge was stitching detection, alignment, fusion, and post-processing into one pipeline.

items: Identity swap plus color correction modules
Detection treated as a survey in the final paper

project-online-kitchen.title: Online Kitchen
project-online-kitchen.subtitle: Interactive visualization project
project-online-kitchen.summary: A Cornell data visualization project built with D3.js around food, nutrition, and production stories.

detail.text: This sat in the Cornell cluster where interface storytelling and data structure started to feel central rather than peripheral.

project-programming-languages.title: Programming Languages Visualization
project-programming-languages.subtitle: Interactive D3 project
project-programming-languages.summary: A Cornell visualization piece exploring programming-language history, family relationships, and popularity through interactive graphics.

detail.text: The project combined history, relation maps, and trend views. In hindsight, it feels especially close to the graph-based personal-site idea.

experience-naec.title: Research Engineer Intern at NAEC
experience-naec.subtitle: National Anti-counterfeit Engineering Research Center
experience-naec.summary: A part-time research internship where a course interest in adversarial examples became sustained work on handwritten signature verification and, later, a journal publication.

detail.text: This was the period when adversarial machine learning stopped being just a course topic and became a real research track. Working with Prof. Wei Yuan's group, I focused on attacking signature verification systems under realistic black-box constraints.

items: Part-time internship during the HUST years
Worked on handwritten signature verification attacks
Fed directly into the RSAEG / region-restricted signature paper

experience-hust-mclab.title: Research Assistant at HUST Media and Communication Lab
experience-hust-mclab.subtitle: Adversarial ML with Prof. Xinggang Wang
experience-hust-mclab.summary: A research-assistant period focused on adversarial robustness, interpretability, and what adversarial training changes inside a model.

detail.text: This lab period deepened the adversarial-ML thread that began with the signature project. Much of the reading, experimentation, and writing around noise modulation came together here.

items: Studied adversarial training and robustness literature in depth
Developed the noise modulation idea around interpretable input gradients
Balanced paper-writing ambitions with unresolved accuracy tradeoffs

experience-realai.title: Software Algorithm Engineer Intern at RealAI
experience-realai.subtitle: AI security internship in Beijing
experience-realai.summary: A short industry chapter in AI security that sat between academic robustness research and later product engineering work.

detail.text: RealAI felt like a bridge. The same adversarial-ML interests appeared in a more applied setting, shaped by industry pace and product constraints. It sharpened the sense that robustness ideas had to survive outside the paper world.

items: Worked as a software algorithm engineer intern
Stayed close to AI security and robustness themes
Helped mark the transition from research-heavy work toward engineering roles

experience-verily-intern.title: Software Engineer Intern at Verily
experience-verily-intern.subtitle: Summer 2022, from Cornell to California
experience-verily-intern.summary: The internship that turned graduate school into a clearer engineering path, connecting Cornell coursework with California life and later full-time work.

gallery.alt: Haoyang in the Bay Area during the Verily internship summer
gallery.caption: Work, relocation, and weekend travel started to blur into one California chapter.

detail.text: This internship marks a real shift in the story of the graph. It is less about papers and experiments, and more about software teams, production thinking, and the rhythm of engineering work in practice.

items: Overlapped with summer travel and the California Route 1 trip
Converted into the full-time role that follows in the graph

experience-verily.title: Software Engineer at Verily
experience-verily.subtitle: Full-time chapter beginning in 2023
experience-verily.summary: The current long-running chapter: product engineering, system ownership, and the part of the graph that connects most directly back to the present-day bio node.

detail.text: Compared with the earlier nodes, this one reflects a steadier and less school-shaped rhythm. It is about longer projects, production responsibility, and a deeper engineering identity that can later branch into writing, side projects, or future moves.

items: Continuation of the Verily internship into a full-time role
Represents the most up-to-date experience thread in the graph
A natural bridge back to the central bio node and present tense

blog-adversarial-writing.title: Adversarial ML Writing Trail
blog-adversarial-writing.subtitle: Zhihu articles, surveys, and dense notes
blog-adversarial-writing.summary: A long writing trail on adversarial examples, robustness, and interpretability, spanning public explainers, research maps, and paper notes.

detail.text: The old site held a large body of writing on adversarial machine learning. Some pieces explained ideas for a broad audience. Others worked more like study notes, mapping papers, concepts, and debates.

items: Zhihu explainers on adversarial examples, adversarial training, and regularization
Long-form note collections such as A Map of Adversarial Example Research
Single-paper and dense-paper notes on robustness, defense, and interpretation

blog-history-writing.title: History Writing
blog-history-writing.subtitle: Essays from outside the technical track
blog-history-writing.summary: A parallel writing thread focused on history rather than engineering, with essays on war, territory, and long arcs of political change.

detail.text: This node keeps the graph from collapsing into only study and work. The old site also made room for essays driven by curiosity about history, geography, and the movement of states and ideas over time.

items: A history essay on the territorial changes of China
A quieter humanities thread alongside technical writing

travel-northeast-2022.title: Northeast Corridor Trips
travel-northeast-2022.subtitle: Cornell-year loops through NYC, Philly, Boston, and upstate
travel-northeast-2022.summary: A cluster of 2022 trips radiating out from Ithaca during the Cornell period, mixing museums, city walks, nearby escapes, and the feeling of learning the U.S. Northeast through movement.

gallery.alt: Haoyang in New York City during the Cornell period
gallery.caption: New York became one of the recurring anchors in that travel season.

detail.text: The old tripline files suggest this was less a single trip than a travel season: New York City in January, Philadelphia in February, Boston in June, and repeated returns to Ithaca and upstate New York through the rest of the year.

items: Ithaca and New York upstate loops across 2022

travel-puerto-rico-2022.title: Puerto Rico
travel-puerto-rico-2022.subtitle: December 2022 island trip
travel-puerto-rico-2022.summary: A late-2022 trip that feels like a marker between the Cornell chapter and what came next, with beaches, coastal stops, and a very different atmosphere from Ithaca.

detail.text: The old tripline artwork turns this into a vivid node: Old San Juan, Punta Higuero Lighthouse, beaches, airport thresholds, and a compact trip at the end of 2022 that feels transitional in the larger story.

items: Playa Escondida and other coastal stops

travel-california-one-2022.title: California State Route 1
travel-california-one-2022.subtitle: A coastal drive during the Verily summer
travel-california-one-2022.summary: A Pacific coast travel node tied to the California internship period, with a scenic route through places like Half Moon Bay, Santa Cruz, Monterey, and 17-Mile Drive.

gallery.alt: Haoyang in the Bay Area during summer 2022
gallery.caption: The Bay was the starting point before the drive turned into a longer coastal day.

gallery.alt: Haoyang in Monterey during the Route 1 trip
gallery.caption: Monterey became the clearest visual memory in the route.

detail.text: Among the old travel artifacts, this one is especially visual. It reads more like a route sketch than a diary entry: coastlines, viewpoints, and a day-long line from the Bay toward Monterey during the Verily summer.

detail.alt: Haoyang in Monterey during the coastal route
detail.caption: A travel node like this benefits from a visual layer even when the written story stays short.
